source_card_summary_title_window: Articles inside selected window · publication rate inside window
source_card_summary_title_total: Total articles for this source · recent publication cadence (last 7 days of articles)
source_card_stat_total_title: All articles ingested for this source. The crawler discovers articles whose sitemap or RSS entry was updated within the last 7 days; individual articles can be older if their listing was recently refreshed by the publisher (re-publications, edits).
source_card_stat_in_window_title: Articles whose publication date falls inside the date range selected on this page.
source_card_stat_freq_title_window: Publication rate inside the selected window: articles in window divided by the window length in days.
source_card_stat_freq_title_total: Recent publication cadence: how many articles per day this source publishes, measured over the last 7 days of its newest articles. Robust against older re-published articles that would otherwise deflate a long-run average.
source_card_stat_no_date_title: Temporal-Provenance-Absence: {count} of {denominator} articles have no real publication date — their timestamp is the crawler fetch time (timestamp_source=fetch_at_fallback). A reflexive disclosure (WP-005 §3.1), not a source defect.
source_card_provisional_title: Source-level classification only — per-article discourse function (Phase 122a) is deferred behind source-level/taxonomy validation; see WP-001 §5.4
source_card_discovery_toggle_aria_hide: Hide per-channel discovery coverage for source: {name}
source_card_discovery_toggle_aria_show: Show per-channel discovery coverage for source: {name}
source_coverage_lede: Per-source emission posture across the Tier-B / Tier-C fields the WebAdapter records. WP-003 §3.2 frames metadata-richness asymmetry as a structural bias; cells flagged as structurally absent reflect the publisher's choice not to emit, not sampling variance.
source_coverage_no_sources: No sources in the probe scope.
source_coverage_summary_meta_title: Fields this source actually populates (≥1 article) out of the full Tier-B/C set
source_coverage_no_observations: No coverage observations recorded yet for this source.
source_coverage_prose_fallback: {source} this source does not emit `{field}` — analyses that require this field exclude it for this source.
source_discovery_lede: Every crawl pass asks each publisher channel (XML sitemap, RSS feed, HTML sitemap pages, date-indexed archive walker) which articles exist in the recent window. This panel shows what the most recent pass actually returned, per channel.
source_discovery_howto_discovered_dd: Raw count of article URLs the channel returned in this pass — before any cleanup.
source_discovery_howto_dedup_dd_lead: Of the discovered URLs, how many were unique to this channel
source_discovery_howto_dedup_dd_tail: . The first channel to surface a URL gets credit; later channels that hit the same URL report it as discovered but not as dedup-unique. Strong overlap (low dedup number for the richer channel) means the publisher's surfaces agree about which articles exist — this is the healthy case. It does NOT mean articles are being skipped; the crawl still fetches every unique URL.
source_discovery_howto_avg_dd: Trailing-window average of the discovered count. Sudden drops vs avg are the publisher changing its discovery surface (sitemap broken, archive truncated, RSS format change) and are flagged as an underflow.
source_discovery_empty: No discovery telemetry yet — first crawl run pending.
source_discovery_underflow_fallback: Discovery underflow active for this channel.
source_underflow_sitemap: The publisher's XML sitemap surfaced fewer URLs than the configured floor. Likely cause: the sitemap moved, the publisher started returning 404, or a service-sitemap-only configuration started filtering harder. Run audit-source-discovery to re-verify the surface.
source_underflow_rss: The publisher's RSS feed(s) surfaced fewer URLs than expected. Likely cause: a feed retired, the catalogue page restructured, or the publisher reduced their feed catalogue. Cross-check the publisher's /service/newsletter or equivalent feeds page.
source_underflow_html_sitemap: The HTML sitemap page surfaced fewer article-shaped links than expected. Likely cause: the page moved, the HTML structure changed, or the article-URL regex no longer matches. Re-derive the `article_url_pattern` from a fresh sample of real article URLs.
source_underflow_archive_index: The date-indexed archive walker surfaced fewer articles than expected. Likely cause: the publisher changed the URL pattern, the date format, or the per-day article volume. Sample two distant dates manually to verify the endpoint still works.
source_coverage_field_prose_published_da: this source does not emit machine-readable publication dates — temporal analyses must rely on heuristic htmldate inference or the fetch fallback.
source_coverage_field_prose_modified_dat: this source does not emit modification timestamps — silent edits remain invisible at the metadata layer (cf. Phase 122d revision-archaeology sidecar).
source_coverage_field_prose_author: this source does not emit per-article authorship — author-level analyses are not comparable across sources whose authorship is asymmetric.
source_coverage_field_prose_description: this source does not emit article descriptions — abstracts must be derived from the full article body where required.
source_coverage_field_prose_categories: this source does not emit categorical taxonomies — topic comparisons cannot use publisher-declared sections from this source.
source_coverage_field_prose_tags: this source does not emit free-form tags — tag-based clustering is sparser here than for sources that do.
source_coverage_field_prose_section: this source does not emit canonical section names — sectional analyses rely on URL-section heuristics for this source.
source_coverage_field_prose_image_url: this source does not emit canonical image references — image-driven analyses are systematically asymmetric.
source_coverage_field_prose_article_type: this source does not emit Schema.org article types — discourse-form analyses cannot rely on publisher-declared types here.
source_coverage_field_prose_word_count: this source does not emit pre-computed word counts — counts are derived from the cleaned body.
source_coverage_field_prose_comment_coun: this source does not emit comment counts — engagement-volume comparisons exclude this source.
source_coverage_field_prose_comment_url: this source does not emit comment thread URLs — discussion follow-through is unobservable from metadata.
source_coverage_field_prose_editor: this source does not emit editorial responsibility — editor-level provenance analyses are systematically asymmetric.
source_coverage_field_prose_reading_time: this source does not emit reading-time hints — must be derived from word count.
source_coverage_field_prose_dateline_loc: this source does not emit datelines — geographic origin analyses are systematically asymmetric.
source_coverage_field_prose_paywall_stat: this source does not declare paywall status — accessibility analyses cannot include this source authoritatively.
source_coverage_field_prose_correction_n: this source does not emit correction notices — silent correction practices are unobservable at the metadata layer.
source_coverage_field_prose_editorial_la: this source does not emit editorial labels (op-ed / commentary / news) — register analyses are systematically asymmetric.
source_coverage_field_prose_external_cit: this source does not emit external citation metadata — cross-source citation graphs exclude this source.
source_coverage_field_prose_images: this source does not emit per-image metadata — multimedia provenance analyses are systematically asymmetric.
source_coverage_field_prose_social_share: this source does not emit social share counts — virality comparisons exclude this source.
source_coverage_field_prose_revision_dat: this source does not emit explicit revision dates — silent revisions remain invisible (cf. Phase 122d).
source_article_empty_filters: No articles found for the current filters.
source_article_modal_tab_empty_title: {source} has no articles for this selection.
source_article_modal_empty_no_source: (No source in the active scope carries this selection.)
source_article_modal_empty_revisions: No articles have revisions in the active window for this scope.
